text.title: Tax Basics for Solopreneurs Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-tax-basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Set aside approximately 30% of net profit into a dedicated tax-savings account MONTHLY. Quarterly catch-up from operating cash is forbidden; the discipline must be monthly.

rationale.source: Solo-operator tax practice (IRS Pub 334; Patrick McKenzie postmortems)

rule.rationale: Operators who postpone set-aside drain operating cash at quarterly estimate dates. Monthly set-aside isolates tax cash before it becomes spendable.

rule.statement: Compute quarterly estimated payments using the IRS safe-harbor rule: 90% current-year tax OR 110% prior-year tax (for AGI > 150k) — whichever is lower. Skipping safe-harbor leads to underpayment penalty.
rule.rationale: Safe-harbor compliance avoids the IRS underpayment penalty regardless of how much the current-year income surprises. The 90/110 rule is the operating floor.

rule.statement: The chosen business structure (sole prop / LLC / S-Corp / C-Corp) is captured in a written decision-record with the trade-offs evaluated (liability, tax, admin burden). Defaulting to sole prop without analysis is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Structure choice affects self-employment tax exposure (S-Corp salary vs distribution split) and personal liability. A decision-record forces the trade-off analysis.

rule.statement: Track deductible expenses in a digital ledger with receipt links (home office %, business travel, software, contractor 1099 payments). Cash-only or memory-only tracking is forbidden.
rule.rationale: The IRS rejects undocumented deductions on audit. Digital tracking is the only practical way to keep deduction integrity over a tax year.

rule.statement: Name the tax-advantaged retirement vehicle in use (SEP-IRA / Solo 401k / HSA) and contribute before the year-end deadline. 'Will set up later' is forbidden as a placeholder for two consecutive years.
rule.rationale: SEP-IRA / Solo 401k contributions are the single largest tax deferral available to solos. Skipping two consecutive years compounds opportunity cost and tax bill.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
